meaning: adj.受诅咒的,要命的,可恨的
examples: And treated like a frigging farm animal!
Why are "web standards" so frigging messed up?
"They're all frigging dinosaurs, " says anesthesiologist Donald Schellpfeffer, who helped start the Sioux Falls Surgical Center, a specialty venue, in 1996.
What Braintree and Square have done is create self-authenticating tokens in a natively multi-merchant construct, and that is a frigging big deal.
